iterations.theme: Putovanje poduzetničkog para: Od osnivanja agencije do obiteljskih izazova

sections.subtitle: Uvod u svijet poduzetničkog para: Jana i Lovro Radeljak
sections.screenshot_description: Marija predstavlja Janu i Lovru Radeljaka, vlasnike Bagadodo PR agencije, dok sjede u studiju.
sections.content: Podcast 'Poduzetništvo s povjerenjem' započeo je uvodom voditeljice Marije, koja je najavila temu bračnih parova u poduzetništvu. Gosti ove epizode su Jana i Lovro Radeljak, vlasnici i direktori uspješne Bagadodo PR agencije, specijalizirane za odnose s javnošću, marketing i komunikacijske aktivnosti. Marija je istaknula da su Jana i Lovro ne samo poslovni partneri, već i supružnici te roditelji troje djece, što njihovoj priči daje dodatnu dimenziju. Na samom početku, Jana je kroz šalu demantirala uobičajenu predodžbu da bračni parovi koji rade zajedno 'cijelo vrijeme tipkaju sinkronizirano i idu na svaki sastanak za ručice', nagovještavajući da je njihova stvarnost mnogo dinamičnija i manje romantična od popularnih mitova.
sections.subtitle_en: Introduction to the world of the entrepreneurial couple: Jana and Lovro Radeljak
sections.screenshot_description_en: Mary introduces Jana and Lovro Radeljak, owners of Bagadodo PR agency, while they sit in the studio.
sections.content_en: The podcast 'Entrepreneurship with Trust' began with an introduction by host Marija, who announced the topic of married couples in entrepreneurship. The guests of this episode are Jana and Lovro Radeljak, owners and directors of the successful Bagadodo PR agency, specialized in public relations, marketing, and communication activities. Marija pointed out that Jana and Lovro are not only business partners, but also spouses and parents of three children, which gives their story an additional dimension. At the very beginning, Jana jokingly debunked the common notion that married couples who work together 'type synchronously all the time and go to every meeting holding hands', hinting that their reality is much more dynamic and less romantic than popular myths.

sections.subtitle: Od Zagreba do poduzetništva: Put Jane i Lovre
sections.screenshot_description: Jana i Lovro pričaju o svom odrastanju i obrazovanju, s fokusom na njihove različite puteve.
sections.content: Marija je započela razgovor pitanjem o odrastanju i obrazovanju gostiju. Jana Radeljak je otkrila da je odrasla u Zagrebu, u brojnoj obitelji s četvoricom braće, što ju je, kako kaže, naučilo borbi i snalažljivosti. Kao najstarija, često je preuzimala ulogu 'šefice'. Kroz cijelo obrazovanje, a posebno na Hrvatskim studijima gdje je završila odnose s javnošću, razmišljala je o pokretanju vlastitog posla. Lovro Radeljak, također rođen i odrastao u Zagrebu, dolazi iz tradicionalne obitelji s jednim starijim bratom. Njegov obrazovni put bio je nešto drugačiji; nakon srednje gimnazije, upisao je brodogradnju, ali je na trećoj godini shvatio da to nije njegov poziv. Preusmjerio se u poslovni svijet, zaposlivši se u prodaji, gdje je stekao mnoge vještine korisne i danas. Dodatno se usavršavao na Akademiji za digitalni marketing i Algebri za web dizajn. Lovro je naglasio da potječe iz poduzetničke obitelji, s majkom knjigovotkinjom i ocem poduzetnikom, što mu je dalo uvid u taj svijet.
sections.subtitle_en: From Zagreb to entrepreneurship: The journey of Jana and Lovro
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana and Lovro talk about their growing up and education, with a focus on their different paths.
sections.content_en: Marija started the conversation with a question about the guests' upbringing and education. Jana Radeljak revealed that she grew up in Zagreb, in a large family with four brothers, which, as she says, taught her to fight and be resourceful. As the eldest, she often took on the role of 'boss'. Throughout her entire education, and especially at Hrvatski studiji where she completed public relations, she thought about starting her own business. Lovro Radeljak, also born and raised in Zagreb, comes from a traditional family with one older brother. His educational path was somewhat different; after high school gymnasium, he enrolled in shipbuilding, but in his third year, he realized that it was not his calling. He redirected himself to the business world, getting a job in sales, where he acquired many skills useful even today. He further specialized at the Akademija za digitalni marketing and Algebra for web design. Lovro emphasized that he comes from an entrepreneurial family, with a mother who is an accountant and a father who is an entrepreneur, which gave him insight into that world.

sections.subtitle: Vizija i hrabrost: Kako je nastao Bagadodo PR
sections.screenshot_description: Jana objašnjava svoju motivaciju za osnivanje Bagadodo PR agencije, naglašavajući potrebu za pričanjem priča.
sections.content: Jana je objasnila što ju je potaknulo na pokretanje Bagadodo PR agencije. Primijetila je velik broj vrijednih projekata, mladih sportaša, udruga i malih brendova koji su smatrali da je PR rezerviran samo za velike tvrtke i budžete. Njezina ideja bila je pokazati da se priča može ispričati za bilo koga, bez obzira na veličinu, te pronaći put do medija i javnosti. Agenciju je pokrenula s 25 godina, bez državnih poticaja, ali uz veliku potporu tadašnjeg zaručnika, a sadašnjeg supruga Lovre, i obitelji. Istaknula je da na početku nije previše razmišljala o preprekama, već je bila vođena željom i podrškom okoline. Agencija Bagadodo PR sada uspješno posluje već jedanaest godina.
sections.subtitle_en: Vision and courage: How Bagadodo PR was created
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana explains her motivation for founding Bagadodo PR agency, emphasizing the need for telling stories.
sections.content_en: Jana explained what prompted her to start the Bagadodo PR agency. She noticed a large number of valuable projects, young athletes, associations, and small brands that believed PR was reserved only for large companies and budgets. Her idea was to show that a story can be told for anyone, regardless of size, and to find a way to the media and the public. She started the agency at 25 years old, without state incentives, but with great support from her then-fiancé, and now-husband Lovro, and family. She pointed out that at the beginning she did not think too much about obstacles, but was guided by desire and the support of her surroundings. The Bagadodo PR agency has now been successfully operating for eleven years.

sections.subtitle: Lovrin skok u nepoznato: Od sigurne plaće do obiteljskog biznisa
sections.screenshot_description: Lovro govori o svojoj hrabroj odluci da napusti siguran posao i pridruži se Janinoj agenciji, unatoč strahovima okoline.
sections.content: Lovro Radeljak pridružio se Bagadodo PR agenciji prije osam godina. Odluka je proizašla iz spoznaje da ga prethodni posao u prodaji, iako koristan za stjecanje vještina i ulazak u poslovni svijet, dugoročno nije ispunjavao. Iako je od početka bio uključen u Janin posao, prilika za prelazak u Bagadodo pojavila se kada je agencija imala dovoljno posla. Unatoč upozorenjima okoline o rizicima rada supružnika u istoj tvrtki i potencijalnoj financijskoj nestabilnosti, Jana i Lovro su se oslonili na **Božju providnost** i vlastitu marljivost. Vjerovali su da će se znati snaći i da će im vjera pomoći u prevladavanju izazova. Marija je pohvalila njihovu hrabrost da napuste siguran posao i uđu u privatni sektor koji tek kreće.
sections.subtitle_en: Lovro's leap into the unknown: From a secure salary to a family business
sections.screenshot_description_en: Lovro talks about his brave decision to leave a secure job and join Jana's agency, despite the fears of those around him.
sections.content_en: Lovro Radeljak joined the Bagadodo PR agency eight years ago. The decision arose from the realization that his previous job in sales, although useful for acquiring skills and entering the business world, did not fulfill him long-term. Although he had been involved in Jana's business from the beginning, the opportunity to move to Bagadodo arose when the agency had enough work. Despite warnings from their surroundings about the risks of spouses working in the same company and potential financial instability, Jana and Lovro relied on **God's providence** and their own diligence. They believed that they would know how to manage and that their faith would help them overcome challenges. Marija praised their courage to leave a secure job and enter the private sector which was just starting.

sections.subtitle: Komplementarne uloge: Jana kao organizator, Lovro kao pokretač
sections.screenshot_description: Jana i Lovro objašnjavaju kako su podijelili uloge u poslu, s Janom zaduženom za planiranje, a Lovrom za operativu.
sections.content: U poslovnom tandemu Jane i Lovre Radeljaka, uloge su jasno podijeljene i komplementarne. Jana se opisuje kao **sistematični tip**, zadužena za organizacijski dio posla, planiranje i vođenje Google kalendara. Lovro je, s druge strane, zadužen za operativu i logistiku, brzo prelazeći s planova na konkretnu akciju. Jana ističe da su dobar tim i da uspijevaju ostvariti zacrtano, često radeći i do kasno u noć. Kada je riječ o donošenju konačnih odluka, Jana priznaje da je kao žena vođena intuicijom i osjećajima, te je manje sklona riziku. Lovro je taj koji često preuzima ulogu **lokomotive** za nove ideje i veće korake, što Jana vidi kao odraz muškog liderstva i lijepe različitosti između muškaraca i žena. Ova komplementarnost, ističu, primjenjuje se i u njihovom obiteljskom životu.
sections.subtitle_en: Complementary roles: Jana as organizer, Lovro as initiator
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana and Lovro explain how they divided roles in the work, with Jana responsible for planning and Lovro for operations.
sections.content_en: In the business tandem of Jana and Lovro Radeljak, roles are clearly divided and complementary. Jana describes herself as a **systematic type**, responsible for the organizational part of the work, planning, and managing the Google calendar. Lovro, on the other hand, is responsible for operations and logistics, quickly moving from plans to concrete action. Jana points out that they are a good team and that they succeed in achieving what was set, often working late into the night. When it comes to making final decisions, Jana admits that as a woman she is guided by intuition and feelings, and is less prone to risk. Lovro is the one who often takes on the role of **locomotive** for new ideas and bigger steps, which Jana sees as a reflection of male leadership and the beautiful differences between men and women. This complementarity, they emphasize, also applies to their family life.

sections.subtitle: Prevladavanje strahova: Od mladosti do poslovne zrelosti
sections.screenshot_description: Jana i Lovro govore o svojim najvećim strahovima u poduzetništvu, uključujući Janin strah od predrasuda zbog mladosti.
sections.content: Jana Radeljak prisjetila se svojih najvećih strahova u poduzetničkim počecima. Kao mlada poduzetnica s 25 godina, bojala se da će biti percipirana kao premlada i da je nitko neće shvatiti ozbiljno na sastancima s visokopozicioniranim osobama. Taj strah je prevladala **stručnošću, znanjem i željom** da uspješno odradi kampanje za klijente. Dolazak Lovre u tvrtku donio joj je dodatnu sigurnost, jer su zajedno dolazili na sastanke. Lovro je govorio o izazovima prekida suradnje s klijentima, no naglasio je da takve situacije vidi kao prilike za učenje. Nakon jedanaest godina poslovanja, više ne razbijaju glavu zbog odlaska klijenata, jer imaju stalan priljev novih upita. Istaknuo je da ne prihvaćaju sve poslove, već odbijaju one koji su suprotni njihovim **kršćanskim vrijednostima**, čak i ako to znači odbijanje potencijalnog prihoda. Marija je primijetila da je PR profesija često percipirana kao 'ženska', no Lovro je istaknuo da mu nije bilo neobično kao muškarcu u PR-u, te da donosi drugačiju perspektivu, posebno u sportskim i tehničkim projektima.
sections.subtitle_en: Overcoming fears: From youth to business maturity
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana and Lovro talk about their greatest fears in entrepreneurship, including Jana's fear of prejudice due to youth.
sections.content_en: Jana Radeljak recalled her greatest fears in her entrepreneurial beginnings. As a young entrepreneur at 25 years old, she was afraid that she would be perceived as too young and that no one would take her seriously at meetings with high-ranking individuals. She overcame that fear with **expertise, knowledge, and a desire** to successfully complete campaigns for clients. Lovro's arrival at the company brought her additional security, because they came to meetings together. Lovro spoke about the challenges of terminating cooperation with clients, but emphasized that he sees such situations as learning opportunities. After eleven years of business, they no longer worry about clients leaving, because they have a constant influx of new inquiries. He pointed out that they do not accept all jobs, but rather refuse those that are contrary to their **Christian values**, even if it means rejecting potential income. Marija noted that the PR profession is often perceived as 'female', but Lovro pointed out that it was not unusual for him as a man in PR, and that he brings a different perspective, especially in sports and technical projects.

sections.subtitle: Majčinstvo i poduzetništvo: Jana o usklađivanju uloga
sections.screenshot_description: Jana objašnjava kako je Lovrin dolazak u tvrtku olakšao usklađivanje majčinstva i vođenja agencije.
sections.content: Jana Radeljak je istaknula da su počeci poslovanja uvijek teški, jer se osim brušenja zanata u PR-u, mora voditi i cijela tvrtka. To je, kako kaže, bilo kao 'dva u jedan'. Veliku blagodat osjetila je kada su krenule trudnoće i majčinstvo, jer je Lovrin dolazak u tvrtku omogućio da se sve stvari provode i komuniciraju. Lovro je tada mogao preuzeti veći dio posla, što joj je olakšalo te faze. Jana je naglasila da su se s vremenom navikli na izazove, te da im je s troje djece većina situacija postala normalna, s uhodanim procesima za usklađivanje obiteljskog i poslovnog života.
sections.subtitle_en: Motherhood and entrepreneurship: Jana about balancing roles
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana explains how Lovro's arrival at the company eased the balancing of motherhood and running the agency.
sections.content_en: Jana Radeljak pointed out that the beginnings of business are always difficult, because besides honing the craft in PR, the entire company must also be managed. That was, as she says, like 'two in one'. She felt a great blessing when pregnancies and motherhood began, because Lovro's arrival at the company allowed all things to be carried out and communicated. Lovro was then able to take over a larger part of the work, which eased those phases for her. Jana emphasized that over time they got used to challenges, and that with three children most situations became normal for them, with established processes for balancing family and business life.

sections.subtitle: Kaos i kava: Život s troje male djece i PR agencijom
sections.screenshot_description: Lovro i Jana s osmijehom opisuju izazove i radosti obiteljskog života s troje male djece, uz šalicu kave.
sections.content: Jana i Lovro Radeljak su roditelji troje male djece: Klare (7 godina), Sare (4 godine) i Tome (godinu i pol). Marija je pitala kako izgleda voditi PR agenciju dok je žena na porodiljnom i neispavana. Lovro je opisao obiteljski život kao 'vrlo zanimljiv, vrlo aktivan, nekad kaotičan'. Jana je, unatoč porodiljnom, uvijek aktivna i dostupna. Oboje su se složili da je vođenje agencije i isporučivanje kvalitetnih usluga klijentima izazovno, ali da se dobro snalaze. Njihov 'tajni sastojak' za preživljavanje je **puno dobre kave**, a Marija je dodala da su njoj u sličnim razdobljima pomagali gumeni bomboni. Lovro je šaljivo dodao da ovisno o dobu dana, rješenje može biti i vino, nakon brojnih kava.
sections.subtitle_en: Chaos and coffee: Life with three young children and a PR agency
sections.screenshot_description_en: Lovro and Jana with a smile describe the challenges and joys of family life with three small children, over a cup of coffee.
sections.content_en: Jana and Lovro Radeljak are parents of three small children: Klara (7 years old), Sara (4 years old) and Toma (a year and a half). Marija asked what it looks like to run a PR agency while the wife is on maternity leave and sleep-deprived. Lovro described family life as 'very interesting, very active, sometimes chaotic'. Jana is, despite maternity leave, always active and available. Both agreed that running the agency and delivering quality services to clients is challenging, but that they manage well. Their 'secret ingredient' for survival is **a lot of good coffee**, and Marija added that gummy candies helped her during similar periods. Lovro jokingly added that depending on the time of day, the solution can also be wine, after numerous coffees.

sections.subtitle: Ključne prekretnice: Od rasta do delegiranja i vrijednosnih projekata
sections.screenshot_description: Jana objašnjava kako je Lovrin dolazak u tvrtku bio ključna prekretnica, omogućivši joj delegiranje i fokus na vrijednosne projekte.
sections.content: Jana je istaknula da je prva velika prekretnica u poslovanju, osim samog početka, bio trenutak kada je Lovro prešao u tvrtku, dvije godine nakon osnivanja. To je bio znak da posao buja i da više nije za samo jednu osobu. Taj korak je donio rasterećenje za Janu i otvorio nove mogućnosti. Priznala je da joj je u početku bilo teško prepustiti dio posla, ali kada je vidjela koliko to dobrih stvari donosi, shvatila je važnost delegiranja. Kroz taj proces, Jana je otkrila i nove Lovrine vještine i vrline. Druga značajna prekretnica je što sada, nakon godina rada, dobivaju mnogo poslova putem preporuka, što im omogućuje da biraju klijente. Posebno su ponosni na rad s **kršćanskim udrugama** i projektima koji su usklađeni s njihovim vrijednostima, poput inicijative 'Poduzetništvo s povjerenjem'. To im omogućuje da rade i za velike tvrtke i za projekte koji im donose osobno ispunjenje.
sections.subtitle_en: Key milestones: From growth to delegation and value projects
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana explains how Lovro's arrival in the company was a key turning point, enabling her to delegate and focus on value projects.
sections.content_en: Jana pointed out that the first major turning point in the business, apart from the very beginning, was the moment when Lovro joined the company, two years after its founding. That was a sign that the business was flourishing and was no longer for just one person. That step brought relief for Jana and opened up new opportunities. She admitted that it was difficult for her at first to hand over part of the work, but when she saw how many good things it brought, she realized the importance of delegating. Through that process, Jana also discovered Lovro's new skills and virtues. Another significant turning point is that now, after years of work, they receive many jobs through recommendations, which allows them to choose clients. They are particularly proud of working with **Christian associations** and projects that are aligned with their values, such as the initiative 'Entrepreneurship with Trust'. This allows them to work both for large companies and for projects that bring them personal fulfillment.

sections.subtitle: Balansiranje posla i obitelji: Svakodnevna borba za prioritete
sections.screenshot_description: Jana i Lovro razgovaraju o izazovima usklađivanja poslovnog i privatnog života, naglašavajući važnost obiteljskih prioriteta.
sections.content: Lovro je potvrdio da posao neizbježno utječe na privatni život. Često se događa da se o poslu razgovara i na privatnim druženjima ili spojevima, jer komunikacija putem poruka i mailova ne prestaje. Ipak, trude se odvojiti posao od obiteljskog vremena, posebno kada su s djecom, kako bi im se u potpunosti posvetili. Jana je naglasila da je to **svakodnevna borba i izazov** u kojem se prioriteti moraju stalno preslagivati, uvijek stavljajući obitelj na prvo mjesto, ne samo teorijski, već i praktično. Priznala je da je posao, ali i kućanski poslovi, mogu obuzeti, te da se mora 'resetirati' kako bi se vratila obiteljskim prioritetima.
sections.subtitle_en: Balancing work and family: Daily struggle for priorities
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana and Lovro talk about the challenges of balancing business and private life, emphasizing the importance of family priorities.
sections.content_en: Lovro confirmed that work inevitably affects private life. It often happens that work is discussed even at private gatherings or dates, because communication via messages and emails does not stop. Nevertheless, they try to separate work from family time, especially when they are with children, in order to fully dedicate themselves to them. Jana emphasized that it is a **daily struggle and challenge** in which priorities must constantly be rearranged, always putting family first, not only theoretically, but also practically. She admitted that work, but also household chores, can overwhelm her, and that she has to 'reset' herself to return to family priorities.

sections.subtitle: Vjera kao temelj: Molitva za strpljenje i mudrost u poslu i obitelji
sections.screenshot_description: Jana i Lovro govore o ulozi vjere i molitve u njihovom svakodnevnom životu i poslovanju, ističući molitvu prije sastanaka.
sections.content: Jana je otkrila da svaki dan započinje **molitvom za strpljenje** i umnožavanje vremena, svjesna da svojim snagama ne može stići sve što si je zadala. Vjeruje da joj takav početak dana pomaže u rasvjetljavanju stvari. Lovro je dodao da se s djecom mole prije svakog obroka i prije spavanja, njegujući te dnevne molitve kao obiteljsku tradiciju. Posebno su istaknuli važnost molitve prije poslovnih sastanaka, čak i u autu, videći to kao pozivanje 'treće osobe' koja ih prati i daje im snagu. Marija je podijelila vlastito iskustvo postavljanja raspela u svojoj poliklinici, naglašavajući kako vjera olakšava i poslovne izazove.
sections.subtitle_en: Faith as foundation: Prayer for patience and wisdom in work and family
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana and Lovro speak about the role of faith and prayer in their daily life and business, emphasizing prayer before meetings.
sections.content_en: Jana discovered that every day she begins with a **prayer for patience** and the multiplication of time, aware that by her own strength she cannot achieve everything she has set for herself. She believes that such a start to the day helps her in clarifying things. Lovro added that he and his children pray before every meal and before sleep, nurturing these daily prayers as a family tradition. They particularly emphasized the importance of prayer before business meetings, even in the car, seeing it as inviting a 'third person' who accompanies them and gives them strength. Marija shared her own experience of placing a crucifix in her polyclinic, emphasizing how faith eases business challenges as well.

sections.subtitle: Izazovi roditeljstva: Kad posao zove, a djeca traže pažnju
sections.screenshot_description: Jana i Lovro s humorom opisuju situacije kada djeca traže pažnju dok oni pokušavaju obaviti poslovne pozive, uz Lovrinu šalu o 'wrestlingu'.
sections.content: Lovro je priznao da se ponekad dogodi da djeci moraju reći 'pusti me, ne mogu sad raditi' kada posao zahtijeva hitnu reakciju, ali se trude biti prisutni s djecom. Istaknuo je da su djeca, posebno kada žele pažnju, sklona tražiti je baš u trenucima kada roditelji trebaju samo minutu mira. Jana je s humorom opisala kako se njezine dvije djevojčice 'bore riječima za pažnju' kada ona i Lovro pokušavaju nešto iskomunicirati, dok kod Lovre kreće 'hrvanje'. Ipak, prednost njihovog zajedničkog rada je što je **uvijek jedan roditelj dostupan** za djecu dok drugi radi. Priznali su da njihov posao ne dopušta strogo definirano radno vrijeme, jer se često javljaju hitnoće koje ne mogu čekati. Klijenti uglavnom poštuju njihove granice, ali intenzivna razdoblja prije projekata zahtijevaju pojačani angažman, nakon čega slijedi zasluženi odmor.
sections.subtitle_en: Challenges of parenthood: When work calls, and children demand attention
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana and Lovro with humor describe situations when children seek attention while they try to make business calls, with Lovro's joke about 'wrestling'.
sections.content_en: Lovro admitted that it sometimes happens that they have to tell the children 'leave me, I can't work now' when work requires an urgent reaction, but they try to be present with the children. He pointed out that children, especially when they want attention, are prone to seeking it precisely at moments when parents need just a minute of peace. Jana humorously described how her two girls 'fight with words for attention' when she and Lovro try to communicate something, while with Lovro 'wrestling' begins. Nevertheless, the advantage of their joint work is that **one parent is always available** for the children while the other works. They admitted that their job does not allow strictly defined working hours, because emergencies often arise that cannot wait. Clients generally respect their boundaries, but intense periods before projects require increased engagement, after which a well-deserved rest follows.

sections.subtitle: Odmor i avantura: Kratki projekti i obiteljska putovanja biciklom
sections.screenshot_description: Jana i Lovro govore o svojim preferencijama projekata i kako planiraju obiteljske odmore, uključujući bicikliranje s djecom.
sections.content: Lovro je izrazio preferenciju za **kraće, intenzivne projekte** s jasnim početkom i krajem, jer nude konkretnije i opipljivije rezultate te sprječavaju 'zamor materijala' koji se može javiti kod dugotrajnih, kontinuiranih poslova. Jana se složila, dodajući da je idealna kombinacija oba tipa projekata. Kada je riječ o odmoru, obitelj Radeljak jako voli putovanja i sportske aktivnosti. Veliki su biciklisti; mlađu djecu voze u prikolici, dok starija Klara već vozi svoj bicikl. Kroz bicikliranje razgledavaju gradove i uživaju u prirodi. Zimi su aktivni na planinama, gdje svi voze snowboard. Često kombiniraju poslovna putovanja diljem Hrvatske s obiteljskim vremenom, pri čemu djeca idu s njima. Oslanjaju se na pomoć Lovrinih i Janinih roditelja za čuvanje djece kada su oboje potrebni za poslovne obveze. Naglasili su da je **planiranje ključno**, iako su svjesni da se ne može sve uvijek odvijati po planu, te da je 20% fleksibilnosti uvijek potrebno.
sections.subtitle_en: Vacation and adventure: Short projects and family cycling trips
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana and Lovro talk about their project preferences and how they plan family vacations, including cycling with children.
sections.content_en: Lovro expressed a preference for **shorter, intensive projects** with a clear beginning and end, because they offer more concrete and tangible results and prevent 'material fatigue' that can occur with long-term, continuous jobs. Jana agreed, adding that an ideal combination of both types of projects exists. When it comes to vacation, the Radeljak family really loves travel and sports activities. They are big cyclists; they ride the younger children in a trailer, while older Klara already rides her own bicycle. Through cycling, they sightsee cities and enjoy nature. In winter, they are active in the mountains, where everyone snowboards. They often combine business trips throughout Croatia with family time, with the children going with them. They rely on the help of Lovro's and Jana's parents for childcare when both are needed for business obligations. They emphasized that **planning is key**, although they are aware that not everything can always go according to plan, and that 20% flexibility is always necessary.

iterations.theme_en: Journey of an entrepreneurial couple: From founding an agency to family challenges

iterations.theme: Dinamika rada bračnog para: Mitovi, balans i duhovno razlučivanje

sections.subtitle: Mit o zajedničkom radu '0-24': Stvarnost bračnog poduzetništva
sections.screenshot_description: Jana i Lovro Radeljak objašnjavaju mit o stalnom zajedničkom radu.
sections.content:
Jana i Lovro Radeljak, vlasnici agencije Bagadodo, razbijaju popularni mit o tome da bračni parovi koji zajedno rade provode 24 sata dnevno, sedam dana u tjednu, sinkronizirano obavljajući sve zadatke. Jana ističe kako ljudi često zamišljaju da oni "tipkaju sinkronizirano, gledaju se cijelo vrijeme, razgovaraju o svim idejama i idu skupa na svaki sastanak za ručicu". Međutim, stvarnost je daleko od toga.

Njihov radni proces više nalikuje štafeti, gdje se izmjenjuju u obavljanju zadataka. Jana radi ujutro, Lovro popodne, ili obrnuto, ovisno o dnevnim obavezama i sastancima. Aktivnosti su podijeljene prema njihovim individualnim talentima i znanjima, što omogućuje nadopunjavanje, a ne istovremeno obavljanje istih stvari. Lovro čak navodi da se ponekad ne vide ni tri dana, unatoč tome što rade u istoj firmi, posebno s obzirom na obiteljske obaveze i dječje sportske aktivnosti. Oboje naglašavaju važnost dnevnog i tjednog "reseta" i odmora kako bi održali ravnotežu.
sections.subtitle_en: The myth of working together '0-24': The reality of marital entrepreneurship
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana and Lovro Radeljak explain the myth of constant joint work.
sections.content_en: Jana and Lovro Radeljak, owners of the Bagadodo agency, debunk the popular myth that married couples who work together spend 24 hours a day, seven days a week, synchronously performing all tasks. Jana points out that people often imagine that they "type synchronously, look at each other all the time, discuss all ideas, and go to every meeting together hand in hand". However, the reality is far from that. Their work process more resembles a relay race, where they take turns performing tasks. Jana works in the morning, Lovro in the afternoon, or vice versa, depending on daily obligations and meetings. Activities are divided according to their individual talents and knowledge, which allows for complementarity, not simultaneous performance of the same things. Lovro even states that sometimes they don't see each other for three days, despite working in the same company, especially considering family obligations and children's sports activities. Both emphasize the importance of daily and weekly "reset" and rest to maintain balance.

sections.subtitle: Pravo na odmor: Uzeti ga, ne čekati
sections.screenshot_description: Lovro objašnjava zašto poduzetnici moraju 'uzeti' odmor.
sections.content:
Na pitanje voditeljice imaju li poduzetnici "pravo na odmor", Jana odgovara da je ključno prihvatiti da je u redu odmarati se. Priznaje da se često bori s time jer joj je "to-do" lista uvijek puna i da se ta lista "konstantno puni, a manje prazni". Ističe da su ženama sve "kutije" (posao, obitelj, kućanstvo) spojene, za razliku od muškaraca koji mogu lakše odvojiti te sfere, referirajući se na poznatu analogiju Don Dabrstojića. Lovro joj često pomaže u tome, potičući je na vrijeme za sebe.

Lovro nadopunjuje Janinu misao, naglašavajući da poduzetnici nemaju "pravo" na odmor, već ga moraju "uzeti". Smatra da je odmor nužan kako bi se izbjegao *burnout* i kako bi se bolje radilo. Ističe da se ne treba uspoređivati s drugima i da je važno postaviti vlastite prioritete, jer "ako se dogodi taj ono čuveni burnout ništa dobro iz toga neće biti, samo i prvi će posao tu patiti".
sections.subtitle_en: Right to rest: Take it, don't wait
sections.screenshot_description_en: Lovro explains why entrepreneurs must 'take' a vacation.
sections.content_en:
To the question of the host whether entrepreneurs have "the right to rest", Jana answers that it is crucial to accept that it is okay to rest. She admits that she often struggles with this because her "to-do" list is always full and that this list "constantly fills up, and empties less". She points out that for women all "boxes" (work, family, household) are connected, unlike men who can more easily separate these spheres, referring to the well-known analogy of Don Dabrstojić. Lovro often helps her with this, encouraging her to take time for herself.

Lovro complements Jana's thought, emphasizing that entrepreneurs do not have "the right" to rest, but must "take" it. He believes that rest is necessary to avoid *burnout* and to work better. He points out that one should not compare oneself with others and that it is important to set one's own priorities, because "if that famous burnout happens, nothing good will come of it, only and first the job will suffer there".

sections.subtitle: Osobni razvoj i efikasnost s dolaskom djece: Nova razina fokusa
sections.screenshot_description: Jana objašnjava kako je majčinstvo utjecalo na njezinu efikasnost.
sections.content:
Jana i Lovro dijele kako se njihov pristup poslu značajno promijenio s dolaskom djece. Jana, koja je agenciju Bagadodo pokrenula s 25 godina, ističe da je kao majka troje djece postala znatno fokusiranija i strpljivija. Prije je mogla "rastegnuti" radno vrijeme na račun slobodnog, no sada, s ograničenim slobodnim vremenom, svaki sat posla je maksimalno iskorišten. "Ako imam dva sata za nešto odraditi, ja ću o tome stići čudo odraditi jer sam baš točno fokusirana, ne odlači me ništa drugo", objašnjava Jana, dodajući da je "Jana sad sa trideset šest godina puno usredotočenija, strpljivija i nekako znam da će uvijek sve doći u svoje vrijeme".

Lovro se slaže s Janom, naglašavajući povećanu efikasnost. Ne želi oduzimati vrijeme obitelji, pa se trudi sve obaviti u kraćem roku. "Nema potrebe rastegnuti cijeli dan za to", kaže Lovro. Oboje ističu da su s godinama i iskustvom postali mudriji u poslovanju, susrećući se sa "sto pedeset različitih situacija" koje su ih naučile kako se nositi s izazovima.
sections.subtitle_en: Personal development and efficiency with the arrival of children: New level of focus
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana explains how motherhood affected her efficiency.
sections.content_en: Jana and Lovro share how their approach to work significantly changed with the arrival of children. Jana, who started the agency Bagadodo at 25 years old, points out that as a mother of three children, she has become significantly more focused and patient. Before, she could "stretch" working hours at the expense of free time, but now, with limited free time, every hour of work is maximally utilized. "If I have two hours to do something, I will manage to do a miracle with it because I am precisely focused, nothing else distracts me," explains Jana, adding that "Jana is now at thirty-six years old much more focused, patient, and somehow I know that everything will always come in its own time." Lovro agrees with Jana, emphasizing increased efficiency. He does not want to take time away from his family, so he tries to get everything done in a shorter period. "There is no need to stretch the whole day for that," says Lovro. Both point out that with years and experience, they have become wiser in business, encountering "one hundred fifty different situations" that taught them how to deal with challenges.

sections.subtitle: Duhovno razlučivanje: Mir kao kompas u poslovnim i privatnim odlukama
sections.screenshot_description: Lovro objašnjava važnost mira u donošenju odluka.
sections.content:
Kada je riječ o donošenju važnih poslovnih i privatnih odluka, Jana i Lovro Radeljak oslanjaju se na duhovno razlučivanje. Lovro ističe da je ključan "mir" u odlukama. "Mi kad znamo da imamo neki nemir, da nešto na skopka, oke, onda se ja zapitam, oke, je li stvarno to, stoje te stvari ili ne", objašnjava Lovro, dodajući da je mir često dobar pokazatelj ispravnosti odluke. Jana dodaje da se često mole, koristeći "trodnevnice ili devetnice" za važne odluke, te da uvijek "izazovemo Duha Svetoga, stvarno da nam prosvjetli pamet kod takvih nekakvih stvari".

Naglašavaju važnost zajedničkog razgovora i razmjene mišljenja, jer "dvije glave su uvijek pametnije". Ponekad izgovaranje problema smanjuje njihovu težinu, a drugi partner može ponuditi novu perspektivu ili ohrabrenje. Lovro spominje da Jana ponekad "emocionalno poleti" za nekom idejom, a on tada unosi kritički pogled, podsjećajući da "ako je nešto predobro da bi bilo istinito, obično i je". Zaključuju da se razlučivanje ne smije temeljiti samo na trenutnim osjećajima, već na unutarnjem miru koji je dar Duha Svetoga.
sections.subtitle_en: Spiritual discernment: Peace as a compass in business and private decisions
sections.screenshot_description_en: Lovro explains the importance of peace in making decisions.
sections.content_en: When it comes to making important business and private decisions, Jana and Lovro Radeljak rely on spiritual discernment. Lovro emphasizes that "peace" is crucial in decisions. "When we know that we have some unrest, that something bothers us, okay, then I ask myself, okay, is it really that, do these things stand or not," explains Lovro, adding that peace is often a good indicator of the correctness of a decision. Jana adds that they often pray, using "triduums or novenas" for important decisions, and that they always "challenge the Holy Spirit, truly to enlighten our minds in such matters". They emphasize the importance of common conversation and exchange of opinions, because "two heads are always better". Sometimes voicing problems reduces their weight, and the other partner can offer a new perspective or encouragement. Lovro mentions that Jana sometimes "emotionally takes flight" for an idea, and he then introduces a critical perspective, reminding that "if something is too good to be true, it usually is". They conclude that discernment must not be based only on current feelings, but on inner peace which is a gift of the Holy Spirit.

sections.subtitle: Spremnost na žrtvu posla za obitelj: Balans nije 50:50
sections.screenshot_description: Jana objašnjava da balans u braku i poslu nije uvijek 50:50.
sections.content:
Na pitanje voditeljice bi li jedno od njih napustilo posao zbog obiteljskih obveza, Jana odgovara potvrdno, bez oklijevanja. Ističe da je važno znati kada napraviti korak unazad, a kada unaprijed. Naglašava da balans u životu nikada nije 50:50, već se prilagođavaju jedno drugome. Ponekad Lovro preuzima više obaveza, ponekad Jana. Smatra da "korak unazad" u poslu često znači "nekoliko koraka unaprijed" za obitelj, jer "to neko ajmo reći mirovanje malo posla i to bude na kraju grupno i naše obiteljski par koraka unaprijed".

Lovro se slaže, ističući da se slobodno vrijeme mora "uzeti", a ne čekati da ga netko da. Navodi primjer svog nogometa četvrtkom navečer kao nešto što je fiksno u rasporedu i što se ne propušta. Oboje naglašavaju da ne funkcioniraju po principu "računice" (ja sam ovo, ti sad moraš ono), već osluškuju potrebe jedno drugoga i djece. Jana prepoznaje kada Lovro treba odmor, i obrnuto, što im omogućuje da se međusobno podržavaju i osiguravaju vrijeme za osobne potrebe i obitelj.
sections.subtitle_en: Readiness for the sacrifice of work for family: Balance is not 50:50
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana explains that balance in marriage and work is not always 50:50.
sections.content_en: To the host's question whether one of them would leave work due to family obligations, Jana answers affirmatively, without hesitation. She points out that it is important to know when to take a step back, and when forward. She emphasizes that balance in life is never 50:50, but they adapt to each other. Sometimes Lovro takes on more obligations, sometimes Jana. She believes that "a step back" in work often means "several steps forward" for the family, because "that, let's say, a little rest from work, in the end, becomes a group and our family's few steps forward". Lovro agrees, pointing out that free time must be "taken", not waited for someone to give it. He cites the example of his Thursday evening football as something that is fixed in the schedule and is not missed. Both emphasize that they do not function on the principle of "calculation" (I did this, now you must do that), but rather listen to each other's and the children's needs. Jana recognizes when Lovro needs rest, and vice versa, which allows them to support each other and ensure time for personal needs and family.

sections.subtitle: Definicija uspjeha i zašto ne zapošljavaju: Obitelj ispred rasta
sections.screenshot_description: Lovro objašnjava zašto ne žele zapošljavati nove ljude u agenciji.
sections.content:
Voditeljica postavlja pitanje o općem mišljenju da se ne može biti dobar roditelj i uspješan poduzetnik istovremeno. Jana odgovara da je "uspjeh relativan pojam". Za nju je uspjeh imati sve životne pozive – supruga, majka, poduzetnica – posložene prioritetno i da svakodnevica to prati. Uspjeh je i kad uspije riješiti jednu stvar s "to-do" liste. Upozorava da će se, ako se mjeri uspjehom svijeta ili drugih ljudi, uvijek biti nezadovoljan, jer "uspjeh je ovaj mjerilo koje si svako za sebe postavlja".

Lovro se nadovezuje, ističući da svatko mora sam definirati uspjeh, a ne slijediti "svjetovne ideale" ili "kontinuirani rast" koji je često problem u poduzetništvu. Njihov uspjeh je što već 11 godina žive od svoje firme, zadovoljni su i mogu uprihoditi dovoljno za obitelj. "Za mene je to uspjeh, da možemo za svoju obitelj uprihoditi ono koliko je nama potrebno i to, za mene je to i više nego dovoljno", kaže Lovro.

**Zašto ne zapošljavaju?**
Lovro objašnjava zašto nemaju zaposlenike. Ne žele ih jer bi to značilo stalno "ganjanje" novih poslova i kontinuirani rast, što smatraju iscrpljujućim i "utrkom koja nema kraja". Njihova vizija je da posao rade sami, a za veće projekte koriste *outsourcing* vrhunskih stručnjaka. Ne žele biti pod pritiskom stalnog osiguravanja plaća zaposlenicima, što bi ih moglo prisiliti da prihvaćaju poslove koji nisu u skladu s njihovim vrijednostima. Njihova vizija uspjeha je da obiteljski život nikada ne pati zbog posla, te da mogu "prikočiti" ili "pojačati" rad ovisno o obiteljskoj fazi. Žele se fokusirati na usavršavanje vlastite stručnosti i pružanje vrhunske usluge klijentima uz pomoć vanjskih suradnika.
sections.subtitle_en: Definition of success and why they don't hire: Family before growth
sections.screenshot_description_en: Lovro explains why they do not want to hire new people in the agency.
sections.content_en:
The host asks a question about the general opinion that one cannot be a good parent and a successful entrepreneur simultaneously. Jana answers that "success is a relative concept". For her, success is having all life callings – wife, mother, entrepreneur – prioritized and for daily life to follow that. Success is also when she manages to solve one thing from the "to-do" list. She warns that if one measures oneself by the success of the world or other people, one will always be dissatisfied, because "success is this measure that everyone sets for themselves".

Lovro adds, emphasizing that everyone must define success for themselves, and not follow "worldly ideals" or "continuous growth" which is often a problem in entrepreneurship. Their success is that they have been living off their company for 11 years, they are satisfied and can earn enough for their family. "For me, that is success, that we can earn for our family what we need, and that, for me, is more than enough," says Lovro.

**Why don't they hire?**
Lovro explains why they don't have employees. They don't want them because it would mean constantly "chasing" new jobs and continuous growth, which they consider exhausting and "a race without end". Their vision is to do the work themselves, and for larger projects, they use *outsourcing* of top experts. They don't want to be under the pressure of constantly ensuring salaries for employees, which could force them to accept jobs that are not in line with their values. Their vision of success is that family life never suffers because of work, and that they can "slow down" or "speed up" work depending on the family phase. They want to focus on improving their own expertise and providing top-notch service to clients with the help of external collaborators.

sections.subtitle: Savjeti za bračne parove: Hrabro, uporno i s povjerenjem u Gospodina
sections.screenshot_description: Jana i Lovro daju savjete bračnim parovima koji razmišljaju o zajedničkom poslu.
sections.content:
Za kraj razgovora, Jana i Lovro Radeljak nude savjete bračnim parovima koji razmišljaju o zajedničkom pokretanju posla. Jana savjetuje hrabrost i odvažnost, ističući da je zajednički posao "nova avantura" koja otvara prostor za bolje razumijevanje i bolji odnos sa suprugom.

Lovro se slaže, dodajući da treba biti realan u procjeni, ali se ne bojati komentara koji sputavaju, poput "pa cijeli dan ćete biti skupa" ili "što ako krene loše". Naglašava da se otkaz može dobiti s bilo kojeg posla, pa ni "najsigurniji" posao ne nudi potpunu garanciju. Ključne stvari za uspjeh su: **samopouzdanje**, **upornost**, **marljivost**, **međusobno povjerenje** i **povjerenje u Gospodina**. Lovro zaključuje da ako se odluka stavi u molitvu i osjeti mir, Bog će je blagosloviti, možda ne onako kako je zamišljeno, ali će "ispasti dobro", jer "Bog zna što nama treba, bolje nego mi sami sigurno".
sections.subtitle_en: Advice for married couples: Courageously, persistently and with trust in the Lord
sections.screenshot_description_en: Jana and Lovro give advice to married couples who are thinking about a joint business.
sections.content_en: For the end of the conversation, Jana and Lovro Radeljak offer advice to married couples who are considering starting a business together. Jana advises courage and boldness, emphasizing that a joint business is "a new adventure" that opens space for better understanding and a better relationship with one's spouse. Lovro agrees, adding that one should be realistic in assessment, but not be afraid of comments that hinder, such as "well, you'll be together all day" or "what if it goes badly". He emphasizes that one can be fired from any job, so not even the "safest" job offers a complete guarantee. Key things for success are: **self-confidence**, **perseverance**, **diligence**, **mutual trust** and **trust in the Lord**. Lovro concludes that if the decision is put into prayer and one feels peace, God will bless it, perhaps not as imagined, but it will "turn out well", because "God knows what we need, better than we ourselves surely".

sections.subtitle: Završne riječi i zahvale: Blagoslov i poziv na dijeljenje priča
sections.screenshot_description: Voditeljica zahvaljuje Jani i Lovri na gostovanju.
sections.content: Voditeljica podcasta zahvaljuje Jani i Lovri Radeljak na gostovanju, iskrenosti i prekrasnoj bračnoj i poduzetničkoj priči. Želi im puno uspjeha, blagoslova, još dječice i projekata. Poziva gledatelje da lajkaju epizodu, pretplate se na YouTube kanal klikom na zvonce te se jave ako imaju zanimljivu poduzetničku i životnu bračnu priču koju bi željeli podijeliti s javnosti.
sections.subtitle_en: Final words and thanks: Blessing and invitation to share stories
sections.screenshot_description_en: The Host thanks Jana and Lovro for their guest appearance.
sections.content_en: The podcast host thanks Jana and Lovro Radeljak for their guest appearance, sincerity, and beautiful marital and entrepreneurial story. She wishes them much success, blessings, more children, and projects. She invites viewers to like the episode, subscribe to the YouTube channel by clicking the bell, and reach out if they have an interesting entrepreneurial and life marital story they would like to share with the public.

iterations.theme_en: Dynamics of a married couple's work: Myths, balance, and spiritual discernment
